name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Floor-alignment should never produce a value greater than the original input.

name: align_result_not_divisible
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_divisible(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFFusize,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of floor-alignment must always be divisible by the alignment boundary.

name: aligned_identity_broken
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_identity_broken(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: If an address is already aligned, align_to_usize should return it unchanged; inconsistency between aligned and align_to_usize would be a spec bug.

name: align_not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_4_align_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFFusize,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(align_to_usize(a, b), b) != align_to_usize(a, b),
{
}
reason: Floor-alignment must be idempotent; applying it twice should yield the same result as once.

name: align_one_not_identity
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_one_not_identity(a: usize)
    requires
        a < 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFFusize,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1usize) != a,
{
}
reason: Every integer is 1-aligned, so aligning to 1 must always return the input unchanged.
